description: You are a mission analysis engineer at a satellite operator. Read the mission requirements document at ~/Desktop/transfer_trade_study_requirements.txt. Your task is to design and simulate two competing orbit transfer strategies in GMAT — a Hohmann transfer (2 burns) and a three-burn bi-elliptic transfer (intermediate apogee at 100,000 km) — to raise the GEOLINK-3 satellite from a 300 km circular equatorial parking orbit to geostationary orbit (GEO: SMA = 42164.17 km, ECC < 0.001) using GMAT's DifferentialCorrector. Save your GMAT script(s) to ~/Documents/missions/ and write a trade study report to ~/GMAT_output/transfer_trade_study.txt. The report must contain exactly these labeled fields: hohmann_total_deltav_km_s, bielliptic_total_deltav_km_s, hohmann_transfer_time_hours, bielliptic_transfer_time_hours, recommended_strategy, deltav_savings_km_s, and orbit_ratio. Login: username=ga, password=password123
